description: Problem: fixtures still import beads_rs::daemon::store_lock internals for lock-file behavior checks. Design: provide typed store-lock test boundary in daemon/surface-owned API (or daemon test utils), retarget fixtures, and tighten visibility on store_lock internals. Acceptance: fixtures no longer import beads_rs::daemon::store_lock internals; relevant daemon/store-lock integration tests pass unchanged.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Implemented store-lock fixture boundary decoupling from daemon::store_lock internals.
Changes:
- `tests/integration/fixtures/store_lock.rs` now uses public path APIs (`paths::store_lock_path` + data-dir override) and local typed error enum.
- Removed direct `beads_rs::daemon::store_lock::{...}` imports.
Validation:
- cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests -- daemon::lifecycle:: (8 pass)
- cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests -- daemon::crash_recovery:: --test-threads=1 (2 pass)
- cargo check -p beads-rs
Acceptance met: fixtures no longer import daemon::store_lock internals and relevant daemon/store-lock flows pass.

title: Extract store-lock fixture boundary API to remove daemon::store_lock internal imports

description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/public_boundary.rs` does not currently assert that the deterministic REPL fault/restart cases moved out of `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs` stay out. The docs are true today, but the boundary guard would not catch drift.

**Design**
Tighten `public_boundary.rs` so it explicitly guards the moved deterministic REPL ownership boundary and fails if those cases creep back into the assembly harness.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `public_boundary.rs` asserts the moved deterministic REPL cases remain outside `integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs`
- [ ] The guard fails for the wrong ownership shape and passes on the intended tree

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/public_boundary.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Closed in /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs on jj change kqlnrpsozozv / commit effd180caa71. Added an explicit ownership note to crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs saying deterministic beads_daemon::testkit::e2e::ReplicationRig coverage stays under crates/beads-daemon/tests/repl/e2e.rs, then tightened crates/beads-rs/tests/public_boundary.rs to enforce that the assembly REPL file does not regrow any non-comment reference to the deterministic ReplicationRig path. Verification: cargo test -p beads-rs --test public_boundary (5 passed). Strong adversarial review on the guard found two brittleness gaps; both were fixed before close.

title: Add public-boundary guard for deterministic REPL cases staying out of assembly harness
